adjudicator_mark.evidence: **Owner A — `pipeline/registry.py`**: F-02 (add `aurora-1` endpoint), F-05 (bump `DEFAULT_RUBRIC_VERSION`), F-09 (fix `DEFAULT_DATASET` path). All three are independent constant edits in the same small file — bundling them in one engineer avoids three people touching the same 11-line file. ... **Wave 2 (area-local application code, still parallel to each other, but each internally sequenced)**: Owner C (rubric.py) and Owner D (run_batch.py) run concurrently with each other — they don't share files — but each owner sequences test-then-fix inside their own file as described above.

votes.evidence: **Wave 1 (fully parallel...)**: Owner A (registry.py) and Owner B (prompt files) run first and concurrently ... **Wave 2 ...**: Owner C (rubric.py) and Owner D (run_batch.py) run concurrently ... Owner C ... F-01 ... and F-11 (add the missing edge-case parsing test)

Wave 1 (fully parallel, no shared files, no ambiguity): Owner A (registry.py) and Owner B (prompt files) run first and concurrently ... Wave 2 (area-local application code, still parallel to each other, but each internally sequenced): Owner C (rubric.py) and Owner D (run_batch.py) run concurrently with each other

disputes.rule: two-of-three majority: the two primary judges' marks plus the adjudicator's mark; because the primaries split one-to-one on a disputed mark, the majority equals the adjudicator's mark, and the mark stays in every denominator
